results.rendered_html:
saṃ-√diś P. -diśati (ind.p. -diśya, q.v.), to point out, appoint, assign,  R. ;  Bhaṭṭ. ;
to state, tell, direct, command, give an order or message to (gen. or dat., sometimes two acc.; with abhi-dūti, ‘to a female messenger’),  MBh. ;  Kāv.  &c.;
to despatch any one (acc.) on a message to (dat.),  Kum.  :
Caus. -deśayati, to cause to point out or declare, invite or ask to speak about,  MBh.

results.rendered_html:
saṃdiś 6 P.
1 To give, grant, assign, make over;  Bk. 6. 141 ;  Y. 2. 232. 
2 To order, direct, instruct, advise, send as a message; kiṃ nu khalu duṣyaṃtasya yuktarūpamasmābhiḥ saṃdeṣṭavyaṃ  Ś. 4 ;  Śi. 9. 56 , 61. 
3 To send as a messenger, entrust with a message; atha viśvātmane gaurī saṃdideśa mithaḥ sakhīṃ  Ku. 6. 1. 
4 To appoint.
5 To commission or depute in general.
